On this page

Types of Computers ​

Not all computers look like the desktop in a lab. A microwave, a smartwatch, and a supercomputer are all computers — they all receive input, process it, and produce output. What differs is their size, power, purpose, and portability.

Grade 10+

Types of computers and their characteristics are introduced in Grade 10. Mobile technology and embedded systems extend into Grade 12.

Classification by Size and Power ​

Microcomputers (Personal Computers) ​

The most common type — used by individuals. All the devices below are microcomputers.

Desktop Computer ​

AspectDetail
LocationFixed on a desk — not portable
ComponentsSeparate: tower/case, monitor, keyboard, mouse
PowerPlugged into mains power
PerformanceHigh — larger components, better cooling, upgradeable
UseOffices, labs, gaming, graphic design
AdvantagesUpgradeable, better cooling, larger screen, cheaper per spec
DisadvantagesNot portable, requires desk space

Laptop (Notebook) ​

AspectDetail
ComponentsIntegrated: screen, keyboard, touchpad in one unit
PowerBattery + power adapter
PerformanceGood — slightly lower than desktop for same price
UseStudents, professionals, home users who need mobility
AdvantagesPortable, built-in battery, all-in-one
DisadvantagesLess upgradeable, smaller screen, can overheat, battery life limited

Tablet ​

AspectDetail
InterfaceTouchscreen — no physical keyboard (can attach external)
OSAndroid, iPadOS, Windows
PowerBattery
UseReading, media consumption, light productivity, education
AdvantagesVery portable, lightweight, long battery life, intuitive touch
DisadvantagesLess powerful than laptop, limited software, typing is slower

Smartphone ​

AspectDetail
InterfaceTouchscreen, voice
OSAndroid, iOS
Connectivity4G/5G, WiFi, Bluetooth, GPS
UseCommunication, social media, navigation, mobile banking, photography
AdvantagesAlways connected, GPS, camera, extremely portable
DisadvantagesSmall screen, limited processing power vs laptop, security risks

Smartwatch / Wearable ​

AspectDetail
InterfaceSmall touchscreen, voice
UseHealth monitoring (HR, steps), notifications, contactless payment
LimitationsSmall screen, limited input, depends on paired phone
ExamplesApple Watch, Samsung Galaxy Watch, Fitbit

Server ​

A server is a powerful computer that provides services to other computers (clients) on a network.

TypePurpose
File serverStores and shares files across a network
Web serverHosts websites, serves web pages to browsers
Mail serverManages email (sending, receiving, storing)
Database serverRuns a database and responds to queries
Print serverManages shared printers on a network
Application serverRuns applications and delivers them to clients

Server characteristics:

  • Runs 24/7 (uptime is critical)
  • No monitor or keyboard usually attached (managed remotely via CLI)
  • Multiple CPU cores and large RAM
  • Redundant storage (RAID) to prevent data loss
  • Runs a Network Operating System (NOS) like Windows Server or Linux

Mainframe ​

AspectDetail
SizeLarge room-filling systems
UsersHundreds to thousands simultaneously
UseBanking transactions, airline reservations, government databases
PerformanceExceptionally reliable, processes millions of transactions per second
ExamplesIBM Z Series

Supercomputer ​

AspectDetail
SpeedFastest computers in existence — measured in FLOPS (floating point operations per second)
UseWeather forecasting, nuclear simulation, climate modelling, drug research
CostMillions to billions of rands
PowerConsumes enormous electricity; requires dedicated cooling
ExamplesFrontier (USA), Fugaku (Japan)

Embedded Systems ​

An embedded system is a computer built into a device for a specific, dedicated purpose. The user doesn't see it as a "computer."

DeviceEmbedded Computer Function
Smart TVProcess remote commands, stream video, display output
Microwave ovenTimer management, power level control
Modern carEngine management, ABS, GPS navigation, entertainment
ATM machineProcess banking transactions, control card reader
Medical pacemakerMonitor heart rate, deliver shocks when needed
Traffic lightControl timing sequence, respond to sensors
Industrial robotPrecise movement control, sensor response

Characteristics:

  • Dedicated single purpose — can't be reprogrammed by user
  • Real-time OS (must respond instantly)
  • Small, energy-efficient, often no display
  • Highly reliable (failure can be dangerous)

Comparison Table ​

TypePortable?Multi-user?Dedicated purpose?Typical Use
Desktop✗✗✗Personal productivity
Laptop✓✗✗Mobile personal use
Tablet✓✗✗Media, education
Smartphone✓✗✗Communication, apps
Server✗✓✗Network services
Mainframe✗✓✗Mass transactions
Supercomputer✗✓✗Scientific computation
Embedded systemVaries✗✓One specific task

Mobile Technology ​

Grade 12 — Mastery

Mobile technology and its advantages/limitations are a Grade 12 focus topic.

Advantages of Mobile Devices ​

  • Portability — take your work/data anywhere
  • Always connected via 4G/5G/WiFi
  • GPS and location services
  • Integrated camera, microphone, sensors
  • Long battery life vs laptop
  • Cloud sync keeps data updated across devices

Limitations of Mobile Devices ​

  • Processing power — less than desktop/laptop
  • Storage — limited internal storage (mitigated by cloud)
  • Screen size — small display limits usability
  • Input method — touchscreen typing slower than physical keyboard
  • Battery life — still limited, needs regular charging
  • Security — more vulnerable to loss/theft; app-based malware risks
  • Connectivity dependence — many features require internet access

4G vs 5G ​

Feature4G LTE5G
Max download speed~150 Mbps~10 Gbps
Latency~30–50 ms<1 ms
Connected devices per areaLower capacityMuch higher density
Use casesVideo streaming, general mobileAutonomous vehicles, IoT, AR/VR

Key Terms ​

TermDefinition
Desktop computerNon-portable personal computer with separate components
LaptopPortable all-in-one personal computer with built-in battery
TabletTouchscreen portable device, usually no physical keyboard
ServerComputer that provides services to networked client computers
MainframeLarge, multi-user computer handling massive transaction volumes
SupercomputerFastest computers used for scientific simulations
Embedded systemComputer built into a device for a single dedicated purpose
RTOSReal-Time Operating System — responds to inputs with no delay
FLOPSFloating Point Operations Per Second — measure of supercomputer speed
4G/5GMobile network standards; 5G is faster with lower latency
WearableComputer worn on the body (watch, fitness tracker)

Exam Focus ​

High-Frequency Questions

  1. "Name the type of computer and give a reason for your answer" — identify desktop/laptop/tablet/server/embedded from a scenario

  2. "Give TWO advantages of a laptop over a desktop" — portability, battery, all-in-one; avoid vague answers like "better"

  3. "What is an embedded system? Give an example." — built into a device for a dedicated single purpose; example must be specific (not just "a computer in a car" but "the engine management system in a car")

  4. "Compare 4G and 5G in terms of speed and latency" — know the numbers and what lower latency enables
